I agree with agent1's selection because *Giardia lamblia* is not only the most frequently encountered cyst-forming parasite in routine stool examinations but also consistently demonstrates robust resistance to environmental stressors, enabling widespread transmission via contaminated water sources—a key factor explaining its dominance in community-acquired gastrointestinal infections.
